Okay, what is the weekly report? A weekly report is a way for you to gain feedback on each teammate as well as the business. What is going well versus where you need to support your team? So what you would do is you would give each person, this is an example of one that you can use that we've also attached as a download, of what you could give each teammate. And then basically I'll show you where the changes would be depending upon the position. So the things that remain static are you always want to have a section of like what went right, where do I need support, what is the data, and what are deliverables, right? And so how this would change, what went right and where do I need support are always just open-ended questions. That's basically a chance for you to give to your teammates, to ask for help, as well as identify their wins so that you can show appreciation, gratitude, possibly give them kudos to the rest of the team, as well as say, oh, okay, this person actually needs more training or they need help from this other person on the team and they're not getting it. And so where do I need support is really what you need to do to help that person win, right? Data is going to be the numbers they're responsible for from the huddles. And so it might be, it would be their seven days of numbers. So sales over the last seven days, closes over the last seven days, no shows over the last seven days, right? That's an easy example. Deliverables are going to be the items discussed on the weekly meeting put into deliverables. So say you assigned Sally to make three new ads and Sally writes down, made three new ads, you know, one was in this spot of the gym, one was over here, blah, blah, gave to Mike to post. Okay. And so that's pretty much it. This is meant to give you a place that they can reflect your teammate, as well as you can gain feedback and insight into their day to day where they need help and where things are going well. Okay. It's as simple as that. Start implementing them right away. These are something that will keep you sane, especially as you grow, because you will feel like you don't have a grasp on what's happening every day. And the only way to keep a pulse on the business is to have people constantly give you new feedback. Okay.
